C[GoKiwi: Nice F4, F4 was the reason why I played at D5 and not C5. I thought C5 to be safer because D5 lets you attach at C5 and secure the corner, but D5 was leaving me the option to play at F4, which you did :D
GoKiwi: Perhaps my C4 is too peaceful
GoKiwi: When stronger players review my games they always say I am very nice to my opponent
GoKiwi: I thought about C3 but some variations were too bad for Black to be tried
okw: C4 is definitely a bit too nice to W
C[okw: C3 is the usual response
okw: the exchange we see there (nobi for w and kosumi for b) typically makes w stronger
GoKiwi: Ah thanks. Yeah, C4 gives the corner for free. You're welcome haha
C[GoKiwi: I now see that my approach was not so good. I think it's most likely that you make a nice wall and by so doing solidify C10.
GoKiwi: I am doing exceptionally favorable exchanges hahah
C[okw: I think it wasn’t so bad, because c10 is low
C[GoKiwi: Yeah, that's not territory yet
C[GoKiwi: I don't like D11 much now
GoKiwi: There are these websites that use Leela or KataGo for player training and that show emojis in place of winrates
GoKiwi: Well for D11 I would think the program would show a very angry face
okw: I think d11 has the good idea (or some good ideas) behind it, but the execution may not be easy
C[GoKiwi: I hope execution will work!
GoKiwi: I was just studying the game with some videos on 3-4 josekis, I felt at ease with the video for 10-19k josekis, and then I accidentally hit the joseki video for 1-2d
GoKiwi: I did not think the 3-4 could be so complicated
C[okw: in my experience the best is to stick to simple joseki until you feel very comfortable (i.e. bored~) with them
